mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength and construction sector activity, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 45%) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO crosses below its 12-month MA (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.5%, real borrowing costs remain negative, prompting equity market repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors demand higher risk premiums.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns show positive mean performance when FEDFUNDS falls below the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption and wage growth, which supports consumer expenditure and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a 60-day return win rate below 40% across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings trajectories, and positive GDPC1 revisions generate immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit no directional bias following positive GDPC1 releases (Rank IC approximately 0.00).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and stable retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and supply chain inefficiencies, which forces corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods exhibiting the INDPRO-RSAFS divergence pattern.
